UPON CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT TAKES THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: CURTIS E.A. KARNOW. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. calendar.matters:
Motion To Dismiss Defendants/Appellants Invalid Appeal Of The Labor Commissioners Order, Decision, Or Award Based On Failure To Post Bond Or Undertaking
Off Calendar Sep-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT ISAAC GERONA'S Motion To Dismiss Defendants/Appellants Invalid Appeal Of The Labor Commissioners Order, Decision, Or Award Based On Failure To Post Bond Or Undertaking.

Respondent Gerona's motion to dismiss is granted. As this court already ruled in its order of July 21, 2023, the bond requirement is mandatory and jurisdictional. (Palagin v. Paniagua Construction Inc. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 124, 140.) Gerona is therefore entitled to a dismissal. (Id.; Labor Code sec. 98.2(b); Burkes v. Robertson (2018) 26 Cal.App.5th 334, 347, fn 10 ["a necessary consequence of subsequent denial of an application [for bond waiver] found by the court to be unmeritorious would be dismissal of the appeal."].)

Petitioner Aurora complains that the Labor Commissioner did not meet certain deadlines and the failure to file the bond was due to circumstances beyond its control. But Aurora cannot avoid dismissal based on such equitable considerations.

"Principles of equity cannot be used to avoid a statutory mandate." (Ghory v. Al-Lahham (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 1487, 1492 [equitable defense could not be used to preclude salaried employee's recovery of overtime compensation given the statutory mandate].)

The matter of attorney's fees can be addressed by a subsequent motion.

A proposed order is emailed the court for review and signature.

Judge: Curtis Karnow, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CK)

Motion To Dismiss Defendants/Appellants Invalid Appeal Of The Labor Commissioners Order, Decision, Or Award Based On Failure To Post Bond Or Undertaking

Notice Of Motion And Defendant'S Motion For Bond Waiver
Off Calendar Jul-21-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:

Theresa Bichsel, Esq., California Department of Industrial Relations, (415) 703-4863, for Plaintiff.

Aceilya Burton, Esq., Fox Rothschild, LLP, (415) 315-2203, for Defendant.

Hearing RE: Plaintiff AURORA RESIDENTIAL CARE HOME INC. and AURORA CONCEPCION's ("movant") motion for bond waiver.

Ruling - ARGUED. The court advises that the reply declaration is inadmissible as it has been signed "under penalty of perjury" under the laws of the State of Nevada. Upon conclusion of argument, the court takes the matter under submission.

Counsel for the prevailing party submitted proposed order via email.

Judge: Curtis E.A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR: 13714, T: (415) 601-7054, E: apourtabib@gmailcom. (302/CK)
